title: The Systematic Removal of Necessary Friction
description: Every major cause shares a common thread: the removal of resistance from life. Some removed friction was oppressive — but some was developmental. Without it, people become fragile.
loop: Technology and culture remove friction from daily life
Discomfort tolerance decreases (never exercised)
Real relationships and commitments feel 'too hard'
Retreat to frictionless alternatives (screens, AI, isolation)
Further loss of tolerance and skill
Deeper isolation and fragility
visualHint: circular arrows getting tighter like a whirlpool pulling inward

title: The Privatization of Everything
description: What was once shared has been made individual — meaning, entertainment, transport, food, childcare, risk, identity, morality. The accumulation leaves nothing requiring genuine interdependence.
loop: Shared activity is replaced by individual consumption (it's more convenient)
Less need for coordination with others
Social skills and tolerance atrophy
Shared activities feel harder (compared to solo convenience)
More retreat to private/individual options
Community infrastructure loses users and funding, declines further
visualHint: shared table slowly splitting into individual trays carried away in different directions

title: The Insecurity–Isolation Feedback Loop
description: Economic insecurity forces self-focus, which weakens community, which removes safety nets, which increases insecurity — a self-reinforcing spiral.
loop: Economic insecurity forces survival-mode self-focus
Self-focus means less investment in community
Weaker community means weaker social safety nets
Weaker safety nets mean more individual vulnerability
More vulnerability means more economic insecurity
Cycle intensifies
visualHint: downward spiral with money symbols at top and a lone figure at bottom

title: The Attention–Loneliness Feedback Loop
description: Technology reduces attention capacity, which makes deep relationships harder, which increases loneliness, which drives more screen time, which further damages attention.
loop: Technology captures and fragments attention
Reduced capacity for sustained presence with others
Relationships become shallower (can't listen, can't stay)
Loneliness increases
More screen time seeking connection or escape
Attention further fragmented
visualHint: phone screen and a person's face circling each other, both getting smaller

title: The Meaning Void → Consumption Loop
description: Loss of meaning creates a void. The market offers products, experiences, and identities to fill it. But purchased meaning doesn't satisfy — so the void returns, demanding more consumption.
loop: Shared meaning frameworks collapse
Existential void emerges (purposelessness, anxiety)
Market offers solutions: self-help, wellness, experiences, identity products
Temporary relief through consumption
Purchased meaning fades (it wasn't earned or rooted)
Void returns, often deeper
More consumption seeking
visualHint: person pouring purchases into a hole that stays empty

title: The Understanding Trap
description: Understanding systemic causes provides validation and relief from shame — but without inner strength-building, it becomes a loop. Sophistication increases while capacity for action doesn't. The person becomes an expert on their own imprisonment without developing the power to leave.
loop: Person struggles (lonely, directionless, fragile)
Discovers systemic explanations (technology, economy, culture did this)
Feels validated — 'it's not my fault'
Relief from shame, but no inner change occurs
Still struggling (because understanding is not capacity)
Seeks more explanation, more frameworks, more validation
Increasingly sophisticated understanding, no more strength
Comfort in diagnosis becomes resistance to the discomfort of change
visualHint: person reading a map while standing still — the map gets more detailed but they never move

title: The Weakening Spiral Across Generations
description: A civilizational feedback loop operating on a 20-30 year cycle. The system neglects mental development, produces undeveloped people, who become the next generation's parents, educators, and system-designers — building systems that further neglect what they themselves never received. Each cycle starts from a lower baseline.
loop: Institutions neglect mental/character development (education focuses on intellect, religion declines, family weakens)
Produces a generation without inner strength — informed but not formed
That generation becomes parents who cannot transmit what they never received
They also become educators, leaders, and system-designers
They build and maintain systems that further neglect mental development (because they don't know what's missing)
Next generation starts from an even lower baseline of inner capacity
Institutions weaken further (staffed by undeveloped people)
The system degrades because its human components degrade — and vice versa
visualHint: spiral staircase going downward — each landing is a generation, each step is lower than the last
